sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Find Level 2 start
level2_start = None
for i, t in enumerate(transitions):
    if t.before_frame.level == 1 and t.after_frame.level == 2:
        level2_start = i
        print(f"Level 2 started after transition {i}")
        break
    if t.before_frame.level == 2:
        level2_start = i - 1
        print(f"Level 2 started after transition {i-1} (first level 2 transition is {i})")
        break

# Look at the frame just before Level 2
if level2_start is not None:
    if level2_start >= 0:
        frame_before = transitions[level2_start].after_frame
    else:
        frame_before = history[0].frame
    seg = frame_before.segmentation
    print(f"\nFrame before Level 2 (level={frame_before.level}):")
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] in ['W', 'G', 'M', 'O', 'P', 'c'] and n['pixels'] <= 30:
            print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Look at first few Level 2 transitions
print("\nLevel 2 transitions:")
for i, t in enumerate(transitions):
    if t.before_frame.level == 2:
        print(f"  {i}: action={t.action}, step={t.before_frame.step}")
        if i > 10:
            print("  ...")
            break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Look at first 10 Level 2 transitions in detail
print("Level 2 transitions (first 15):")
for i, t in enumerate(transitions):
    if t.before_frame.level == 2 and i < 90:
        seg_before = t.before_frame.segmentation
        seg_after = t.after_frame.segmentation
        
        # Find white and dark gray before
        white_before = [n for n in seg_before['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 1]
        dark_before = [n for n in seg_before['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] == 12]
        
        # Find white and dark gray after
        white_after = [n for n in seg_after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 1]
        dark_after = [n for n in seg_after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] == 12]
        
        print(f"\n  Transition {i}: action={t.action}, step={t.before_frame.step}")
        if white_before:
            print(f"    White before: {white_before[0]['boundary']}")
        if white_after:
            print(f"    White after: {white_after[0]['boundary']}")
        if i > 14:
            break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Look at first 10 Level 2 transitions in detail
print("Level 2 transitions (first 15):")
for i, t in enumerate(transitions):
    if t.before_frame.level == 2 and i < 90:
        seg_before = t.before_frame.segmentation
        seg_after = t.after_frame.segmentation
        
        # Find white and dark gray before
        white_before = [n for n in seg_before['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 1]
        dark_before = [n for n in seg_before['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] == 12]
        
        # Find white and dark gray after
        white_after = [n for n in seg_after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 1]
        dark_after = [n for n in seg_after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] == 12]
        
        print(f"\n  Transition {i}: action={t.action}, step={t.before_frame.step}")
        if white_before:
            print(f"    White before: {white_before[0]['boundary']}")
        if white_after:
            print(f"    White after: {white_after[0]['boundary']}")
        if i > 14:
            break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
